disclaimer: Agency guidelines sourced from public federal documents. Lender overlay categories represent typical market ranges — not specific named lenders. Actual lender requirements vary and change frequently. Always verify directly with your lender.

lenders.name: Fannie Mae (Agency Standard)
lenders.note: Public guideline: Fannie Mae Selling Guide (fanniemae.com)

lenders.name: Freddie Mac (Agency Standard)
lenders.note: Public guideline: Freddie Mac Seller/Servicer Guide (freddiemac.com)

lenders.name: VA Loan (Standard)
lenders.note: Public guideline: VA Lender's Handbook Chapter 4 (benefits.va.gov)

lenders.name: Conservative Bank Overlay (Typical)
lenders.note: Represents typical conservative lender overlay. Actual requirements vary by institution.

lenders.name: Moderate Bank Overlay (Typical)
lenders.note: Represents typical moderate lender overlay. Actual requirements vary by institution.

lenders.name: Flexible Non-Bank Overlay (Typical)
lenders.note: Represents flexible non-bank lender overlay. Actual requirements vary by institution.

lenders.name: Physician Loan Program (Typical)
lenders.note: Typical physician loan program terms. MD/DO/DDS/PharmD. Offer letter accepted. No PMI.

lenders.name: Bank Statement Loan / Non-QM (Typical)
lenders.note: 12-24 months bank statements. 50% expense factor typical. Higher rate than QM loans.

lenders.name: DSCR Investor Loan (Typical)
lenders.note: No income verification — rental income covers PITI. Investment properties only.

lenders.name: Asset Depletion (Typical)
lenders.note: Assets / 360 months = qualifying income. High net worth borrowers.

lenders.name: Credit Union (Typical)
lenders.note: Credit unions often follow agency guidelines closely. Member benefits may apply.

lenders.name: Portfolio Lender (Typical)
lenders.note: Portfolio lenders hold loans on own books. Most flexible — manual underwriting common.
